title: ED back pain documentation
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

red_flags.text: Lists weight loss, fever, night sweats, malignancy, osteoporosis, weakness, gait instability, sensory change, bowel or bladder dysfunction, trauma, infection, injection drugs, corticosteroids, and recent spinal procedures.
red_flags.rationale: The exact red-flag section supports recording time-critical symptoms only when the clinician assessed them.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists weight loss, fever, night sweats, malignancy, osteoporosis, weakness, gait instability, sensory change, bowel or bladder dysfunction, trauma, infection, injection drugs, corticosteroids, and recent spinal procedures.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Acute Low Back Pain – 15

red_flags.text: Defines urgent and specialist review for severe or night pain, infection, trauma, neurological deficit, urinary or faecal dysfunction, systemic disease, persistent symptoms, and selected imaging needs.
red_flags.rationale: The exact referral section supports recording escalation, specialist discussion or arranged follow-up only when clinician-decided.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Defines urgent and specialist review for severe or night pain, infection, trauma, neurological deficit, urinary or faecal dysfunction, systemic disease, persistent symptoms, and selected imaging needs.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Acute Low Back Pain – 15

investigations.text: Describes cauda-equina symptom combinations and limits imaging in short-duration low back pain without associated concern features.
investigations.rationale: The exact cauda-equina and imaging section supports documenting existing imaging or test status only when already performed or reviewed, without recommending imaging.
investigations.evidence_extract: Describes cauda-equina symptom combinations and limits imaging in short-duration low back pain without associated concern features.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Acute Low Back Pain – 15

treatment_and_management.text: Supports acute low-back-pain terminology and pain site, onset, duration, severity, character, radiation, aggravating factors, injury mechanism, prior treatment, function, and patient concerns.
treatment_and_management.rationale: The exact DHA acute low-back-pain history section supports onset, duration, severity, functional impact, associated symptoms, relevant negatives and risk context without asserting a cause.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports acute low-back-pain terminology and pain site, onset, duration, severity, character, radiation, aggravating factors, injury mechanism, prior treatment, function, and patient concerns.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Acute Low Back Pain – 15

source_limitations: Trauma, location/radiation, neurological and bladder/bowel detail, vascular or abdominal causes, examination, diagnosis, tests, treatment, reassessment and disposition remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
